06-Exercices-GARCH-VaR

Author

Romain CAPLIEZ

Exercice 1 : Modéliser le risque du CAC40

Données à partir de la base Yahoo Finance : fonction pdfetch::pdfetch_YAHOO().

Vous avez pour mission de modéliser le risque présent sur le marché du CAC40.

Question 0

Importer le setup de l’analyse.

Question 1

Importer les données du CAC40 à partir de la base Yahoo Finance.

Question 2

Les données de cours boursier du CAC40 sont-elles stationnaires ?

Question 3

Stationnariser la série si besoin

Question 4

Modéliser le risque à partir d’une mesure de Value at Risk non-conditionnelle historique, de Student (normale pour une version plus simple) en statique et en fenêtre glissante de 252 jours. Commentez.

Question 5

Trouver le meilleur modèle (S)ARIMA pour modéliser l’espérance conditionnelle de la série stationnaire à partir du critère d’information BIC

Question 6

Les résidus de ce modèle sont-ils normaux ? Sont-ils auto-corrélés ? Les résidus au carré sont-ils auto-corrélés ? Y a-t-il des effets ARCH dans les résidus. Qu’en concluez-vous pour la suite de l’analyse.

Question 7

Estimer la volatilité conditionnelle à partir de 3 modèles (minimum) différents. Expliquez votre processus de sélection et de validation des modèles utilisés.

Question 8

Calculer la VaR associée à ces 3 modèles.

Question 9

Effectuer les tests de Backtests sur tous les modèles de VaR estimés. Quelles sont les conclusions ?

Question 10

Parmi tous les modèles de VaR validés (ou parmi tous les modèles s’il n’y a pas assez de modèles validés) quel est le modèle que vous choisissez pour modéliser le risque du CAC40 ?

Exercice 2 : Prédire le FTSE100

Votre patron vous demande de prédire les futurs rendements du FTSE100 ainsi que les risques de perte maximum pour un niveau de confiance de 99%. Expliquer votre démarche et abalyser les résultats.

Exercice 3 : LVMH

Votre collège part à la retraite et vous transmet le projet sur lequel il travaillait : modéliser le risque des prix de l’action LVMH. Commentez les résultats et les méthodes qu’il utilise.

rm(list = ls())
source(here::here("02-codes", "utils", "setup.R"))
lvmh <- 
  pdfetch::pdfetch_YAHOO(
    "MC.PA",
    fields = "adjclose", 
    from = as.Date("2008-01-03"), 
    to = as.Date("2026-03-27") 
  )
forecast::ggtsdisplay(lvmh, point = FALSE)
plot(lvmh)
urca::ur.df(lvmh, "trend", lags = 20, selectlags = "AIC") |> summary()

############################################### 
# Augmented Dickey-Fuller Test Unit Root Test # 
############################################### 

Test regression trend 


Call:
lm(formula = z.diff ~ z.lag.1 + 1 + tt + z.diff.lag)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-46.415  -1.581  -0.043   1.628  83.432 

Coefficients:
               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)   
(Intercept)  -0.0069552  0.2142287  -0.032  0.97410

tt            0.0002516  0.0001712   1.470  0.14173   
z.diff.lag1  -0.0154563  0.0146999  -1.051  0.29310   
z.diff.lag2   0.0103342  0.0146878   0.704  0.48173   
z.diff.lag3  -0.0393154  0.0146881  -2.677  0.00746 **
z.diff.lag4  -0.0074513  0.0146900  -0.507  0.61201   
z.diff.lag5   0.0134215  0.0146851   0.914  0.36079   
z.diff.lag6  -0.0062564  0.0146876  -0.426  0.67016

z.diff.lag10 -0.0079604  0.0147054  -0.541  0.58831   
z.diff.lag11  0.0106391  0.0147066   0.723  0.46946   
z.diff.lag12  0.0121688  0.0147075   0.827  0.40806   
z.diff.lag13 -0.0250169  0.0147090  -1.701  0.08905 . 
z.diff.lag14  0.0423764  0.0147134   2.880  0.00399 **
z.diff.lag15 -0.0126623  0.0147144  -0.861  0.38954   
z.diff.lag16 -0.0415920  0.0147152  -2.826  0.00473 **
z.diff.lag17 -0.0400166  0.0147267  -2.717  0.00661 **

      1pct  5pct 10pct
tau3 -3.96 -3.41 -3.12
phi2  6.09  4.68  4.03
phi3  8.27  6.25  5.34
urca::ur.df(lvmh, "drift", lags = 20, selectlags = "AIC") |> summary()

# Augmented Dickey-Fuller Test Unit Root Test # 
############################################### 

Test regression drift 


Call:
lm(formula = z.diff ~ z.lag.1 + 1 + z.diff.lag)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-46.265  -1.577  -0.065   1.659  83.381 

Coefficients:
               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)   
(Intercept)   0.2145522  0.1522562   1.409  0.15886   
z.lag.1      -0.0004062  0.0004073  -0.997  0.31869   
z.diff.lag1  -0.0162387  0.0146921  -1.105  0.26910   
z.diff.lag2   0.0095839  0.0146808   0.653  0.51390   
z.diff.lag3  -0.0400535  0.0146813  -2.728  0.00639 **
z.diff.lag4  -0.0082081  0.0146828  -0.559  0.57617

z.diff.lag6  -0.0069929  0.0146809  -0.476  0.63387

urca::ur.df(lvmh, "none", lags = 20, selectlags = "AIC") |> summary()

############################################### 
# Augmented Dickey-Fuller Test Unit Root Test # 
############################################### 

Test regression none 


Call:
lm(formula = z.diff ~ z.lag.1 - 1 + z.diff.lag)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-46.310  -1.453   0.080   1.786  83.306 

Coefficients:
                Estimate  Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)   
z.lag.1       0.00003509  0.00026050   0.135  0.89284   
z.diff.lag1  -0.01625228  0.01469361  -1.106  0.26875   
z.diff.lag2   0.00957592  0.01468237   0.652  0.51430   
z.diff.lag3  -0.04006456  0.01468290  -2.729  0.00638 **
z.diff.lag4  -0.00820225  0.01468438  -0.559  0.57648   
z.diff.lag5   0.01269758  0.01468000   0.865  0.38711   
z.diff.lag6  -0.00698518  0.01468245  -0.476  0.63427   
z.diff.lag7   0.00967311  0.01468272   0.659  0.51005   
z.diff.lag8   0.00359892  0.01468450   0.245  0.80640   
z.diff.lag9   0.00676191  0.01468465   0.460  0.64520   
z.diff.lag10 -0.00865646  0.01470120  -0.589  0.55601   
z.diff.lag11  0.00995664  0.01470260   0.677  0.49831   
z.diff.lag12  0.01148835  0.01470364   0.781  0.43465   
z.diff.lag13 -0.02572320  0.01470475  -1.749  0.08030 . 
z.diff.lag14  0.04170735  0.01470964   2.835  0.00460 **
z.diff.lag15 -0.01333822  0.01471095  -0.907  0.36462   
z.diff.lag16 -0.04225736  0.01471184  -2.872  0.00409 **

---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 6.623 on 4626 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.008667,  Adjusted R-squared:  0.00481 
F-statistic: 2.247 on 18 and 4626 DF,  p-value: 0.001876


Value of test-statistic is: 0.1347 

Critical values for test statistics: 
      1pct  5pct 10pct
tau1 -2.58 -1.95 -1.62
rend_lvmh <- 
  log(lvmh) - log(lag(lvmh)) |> 
  na.omit()

forecast::ggtsdisplay(rend_lvmh, point = FALSE)
moments::jarque.test(as.numeric(rend_lvmh))

    Jarque-Bera Normality Test

data:  as.numeric(rend_lvmh)
JB = 4287, p-value < 0.00000000000000022
alternative hypothesis: greater
t_couv <- 0.01
VaR_VCov <- (-(mean(rend_lvmh)+sd(rend_lvmh)*qnorm(t_couv))) |> print()
[1] 0.04291615
plot(as.zoo(rend_lvmh),
      main = "Rendements et Value at Risk variance-covariance",
      xlab = "Temps",
      ylab = "Rendements")
  abline(h= -VaR_VCov, col="cornflowerblue")
  points(as.zoo(rend_lvmh[as.numeric(rend_lvmh) < -VaR_VCov]), pch=20, col="cornflowerblue")
  legend("topright", legend = c("VaR Vcov","Dépassements de la VaR Vcov"), 
         pch = c(NA,20), lty = c(1, NA), col = "cornflowerblue")
K <- kurtosis(rend_lvmh)
S <- skewness(rend_lvmh)

k <- as.numeric(qnorm(t_couv)+(qnorm(t_couv)^2-1)*(S/6)+(qnorm(t_couv)^3-3*qnorm(t_couv))*(K/24)+(2*qnorm(t_couv)^3-5*qnorm(t_couv))*(S^2/36))

VaR_CFisher<- as.numeric(-(mean(rend_lvmh)+sd(rend_lvmh)*k)) |> print()
[1] 0.07455509
plot(as.zoo(rend_lvmh),
      main = "Rendements et Value at Risk Cornish-Fisher",
      xlab = "Temps",
      ylab = "Rendements")
  abline(h= -VaR_CFisher, col="mediumorchid")
  points(as.zoo(rend_lvmh[as.numeric(rend_lvmh) < -VaR_CFisher]), pch=20, col="mediumorchid")
  legend("topright", legend = c("VaR CFisher","Dépassements de la VaR CFisher"), 
         pch = c(NA,20), lty = c(1, NA), col = "mediumorchid")
param.sd <- fitdist(distribution = 'std', x = rend_lvmh)$pars
quantile.sd <- qt(t_couv, df=param.sd["shape"])

VaR_student <- (-mean(rend_lvmh)-sd(rend_lvmh)*quantile.sd*sqrt((quantile.sd-2)/quantile.sd)) |> print()
[1] 0.0885578
plot(as.zoo(rend_lvmh),
      main = "Rendements et Value at Risk Student",
      xlab = "Temps",
      ylab = "Rendements")
  abline(h= -VaR_student, col="mediumseagreen")
  points(as.zoo(rend_lvmh[as.numeric(rend_lvmh) < -VaR_student]), pch=20, col="mediumseagreen")
  legend("topright", legend = c("VaR Student","Dépassements de la VaR Student"), 
         pch = c(NA,20), lty = c(1, NA), col = "mediumseagreen")
n_backtest <- 2000

taille_sample_estimation <- length(rend_lvmh) - n_backtest

sample_estimation <- rend_lvmh[1:taille_sample_estimation]

sample_backtest <- rend_lvmh[(taille_sample_estimation+1):length(rend_lvmh)]
VaR_VCov_estimation <- (-(mean(sample_estimation)+sd(sample_estimation)*qnorm(t_couv)))

test_VaR_VCov <- 
  rugarch::VaRTest(
    alpha = t_couv, 
    actual = sample_backtest, 
    VaR = rep(-VaR_VCov_estimation, times = length(sample_backtest)),
    conf.level = 0.95 
  )

tibble(
  name = names(unlist(test_VaR_VCov)), 
  value = unlist(test_VaR_VCov) 
) |> 
  print(n = 20)
# A tibble: 12 × 2
   name            value                            
   <chr>           <chr>                            
 1 expected.exceed 20                               
 2 actual.exceed   34                               
 3 uc.H0           Correct Exceedances              
 4 uc.LRstat       8.18194510909123                 
 5 uc.critical     3.84145882069412                 
 6 uc.LRp          0.00423093684953424              
 7 uc.Decision     Reject H0                        
 8 cc.H0           Correct Exceedances & Independent
 9 cc.LRstat       10.4255909694824                 
10 cc.critical     5.99146454710798                 
11 cc.LRp          0.00544642698307951              
12 cc.Decision     Reject H0                        
HIT_VCov <- 
  if_else(sample_backtest < -VaR_VCov_estimation, 1, 0)

HIT_centre_VCov <- HIT_VCov - t_couv

stats::Box.test(HIT_centre_VCov, lag = 5, type = "Ljung-Box")

    Box-Ljung test

data:  HIT_centre_VCov
X-squared = 28.608, df = 5, p-value = 0.00002767
df <- tibble(
  HIT_centre = HIT_centre_VCov, 
  rend = sample_backtest |> as.numeric(), 
  rend2 = sample_backtest**2 |> as.numeric()
) 

ARDL::ardl( 
  HIT_centre ~ rend + rend2, 
  data = df,
  order = 5 
) |> 
  summary()

Time series regression with "ts" data:
Start = 6, End = 2000

Call:
dynlm::dynlm(formula = full_formula, data = data, start = start, 
    end = end)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-0.71827 -0.02552  0.00925  0.02990  0.73041 

Coefficients:
                  Estimate Std. Error t value             Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)      -0.018914   0.003056  -6.190       0.000000000731 ***
L(HIT_centre, 1)  0.003505   0.022494   0.156               0.8762    
L(HIT_centre, 2) -0.016735   0.022497  -0.744               0.4571    
L(HIT_centre, 3)  0.017929   0.022536   0.796               0.4264    
L(HIT_centre, 4) -0.004361   0.022506  -0.194               0.8464    
L(HIT_centre, 5)  0.004296   0.022482   0.191               0.8485    
rend             -3.152928   0.120388 -26.190 < 0.0000000000000002 ***
L(rend, 1)       -0.185045   0.139890  -1.323               0.1861    
L(rend, 2)       -0.317955   0.139848  -2.274               0.0231 *  
L(rend, 3)        0.058905   0.140008   0.421               0.6740    
L(rend, 4)        0.021289   0.140771   0.151               0.8798    
L(rend, 5)       -0.024330   0.140435  -0.173               0.8625    
rend2            76.422489   2.662759  28.700 < 0.0000000000000002 ***
L(rend2, 1)       0.211921   3.165889   0.067               0.9466    
L(rend2, 2)       6.670528   3.144204   2.122               0.0340 *  
L(rend2, 3)       0.909246   3.139156   0.290               0.7721    
L(rend2, 4)      -2.119299   3.147435  -0.673               0.5008    
L(rend2, 5)      -3.543890   3.145991  -1.126               0.2601    
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 0.09867 on 1977 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.4241,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.4192 
F-statistic: 85.64 on 17 and 1977 DF,  p-value: < 0.00000000000000022
K <- kurtosis(sample_estimation)
S <- skewness(sample_estimation)

k <- as.numeric(qnorm(t_couv)+(qnorm(t_couv)^2-1)*(S/6)+(qnorm(t_couv)^3-3*qnorm(t_couv))*(K/24)+(2*qnorm(t_couv)^3-5*qnorm(t_couv))*(S^2/36))

VaR_CFisher_estimation <- as.numeric(-(mean(sample_estimation)+sd(sample_estimation)*k))

test_VaR_CFisher <- 
  rugarch::VaRTest(
    alpha = t_couv, 
    actual = sample_backtest, 
    VaR = rep(-VaR_CFisher_estimation, times = length(sample_backtest)),
    conf.level = 0.95 
  )

tibble(
  name = names(unlist(test_VaR_CFisher)), 
  value = unlist(test_VaR_CFisher) 
) |> 
  print(n = 20)
# A tibble: 12 × 2
   name            value                            
   <chr>           <chr>                            
 1 expected.exceed 20                               
 2 actual.exceed   4                                
 3 uc.H0           Correct Exceedances              
 4 uc.LRstat       19.2534427663781                 
 5 uc.critical     3.84145882069412                 
 6 uc.LRp          0.0000114463780930274            
 7 uc.Decision     Reject H0                        
 8 cc.H0           Correct Exceedances & Independent
 9 cc.LRstat       19.2694828773756                 
10 cc.critical     5.99146454710798                 
11 cc.LRp          0.0000654161466900893            
12 cc.Decision     Reject H0                        
HIT_CFisher <- 
  if_else(sample_backtest < -VaR_CFisher_estimation, 1, 0)

HIT_centre_CFisher <- HIT_VCov - t_couv

stats::Box.test(HIT_centre_VCov, lag = 5, type = "Ljung-Box")

    Box-Ljung test

data:  HIT_centre_VCov
X-squared = 28.608, df = 5, p-value = 0.00002767
df <- tibble(
  HIT_centre = HIT_centre_CFisher, 
  rend = sample_backtest |> as.numeric(), 
  rend2 = sample_backtest**2 |> as.numeric()
) 

ARDL::ardl( 
  HIT_centre ~ rend + rend2, 
  data = df,
  order = 5 
) |> 
  summary()

Time series regression with "ts" data:
Start = 6, End = 2000

    end = end)

param.sd <- fitdist(distribution = 'std', x = sample_estimation)$pars
quantile.sd <- qt(t_couv, df=param.sd["shape"])

VaR_student_estimation <- (-mean(sample_estimation)-sd(sample_estimation)*quantile.sd*sqrt((quantile.sd-2)/quantile.sd))

test_VaR_student <- 
  rugarch::VaRTest(
    alpha = t_couv, 
    actual = sample_backtest, 
    VaR = rep(-VaR_student_estimation, times = length(sample_backtest)),
    conf.level = 0.95 
  )

tibble(
  name = names(unlist(test_VaR_student)), 
  value = unlist(test_VaR_student) 
) |> 
  print(n = 20)
# A tibble: 12 × 2
   name            value                            
   <chr>           <chr>                            
 1 expected.exceed 20                               
 2 actual.exceed   1                                
 3 uc.H0           Correct Exceedances              
 4 uc.LRstat       32.1902782785452                 
 5 uc.critical     3.84145882069412                 
 6 uc.LRp          0.0000000139788461916979         
 7 uc.Decision     Reject H0                        
 8 cc.H0           Correct Exceedances & Independent
 9 cc.LRstat       32.1912792795877                 
10 cc.critical     5.99146454710798                 
11 cc.LRp          0.000000102271004553955          
12 cc.Decision     Reject H0                        
HIT_student <- 
  if_else(sample_backtest < -VaR_student_estimation, 1, 0)

HIT_centre_student <- HIT_VCov - t_couv

stats::Box.test(HIT_centre_student, lag = 5, type = "Ljung-Box")

    Box-Ljung test

data:  HIT_centre_student
X-squared = 28.608, df = 5, p-value = 0.00002767
df <- tibble(
  HIT_centre = HIT_centre_student, 
  rend = sample_backtest |> as.numeric(), 
  rend2 = sample_backtest**2 |> as.numeric()
) 

ARDL::ardl( 
  HIT_centre ~ rend + rend2, 
  data = df,
  order = 5 
) |> 
  summary()

    end = end)

best_arma <- forecast::auto.arima(rend_lvmh)

ar <- best_arma$arma[1]
ma <- ar <- best_arma$arma[2]

best_arma$arma[c(1,6,2)] |>  print()
[1] 1 0 2
LSTS::Box.Ljung.Test(best_arma$residuals**2, lag = 40)
fDMA::archtest(as.numeric(best_arma$residuals**2), lag = 20)

    Engle's LM ARCH Test

data:  as.numeric(best_arma$residuals^2)
statistic = 213.21, lag = 20, p-value < 0.00000000000000022
alternative hypothesis: ARCH effects of order 20 are present
garch11_spec <- 
  rugarch::ugarchspec(
    variance.model = 
      list(
        model = "sGARCH", 
        garchOrder = c(1,1),
        variance.targeting = TRUE 
      ),
    mean.model = 
      list(
        armaOrder = c(ar,ma), 
        include.mean = TRUE 
      ),
    distribution.model = "sged" 
  )

garch11_fit <- 
  rugarch::ugarchfit(
    garch11_spec, 
    rend_lvmh 
  )

garch11_fit

*---------------------------------*
*          GARCH Model Fit        *
*---------------------------------*

Conditional Variance Dynamics   
-----------------------------------
GARCH Model : sGARCH(1,1)
Mean Model  : ARFIMA(2,0,2)
Distribution    : sged

------------------------------------

Elapsed time : 2.516509 
plot(garch11_fit, which = 9)
# Calculer la VaR à partir du modèle EGARCH
VaR_GARCH <- -(as.numeric(quantile(garch11_fit, probs = t_couv)))

# Représenter les rendements et la VaR
plot(
  # Combiner dans un seul df les rendements et la VaR
  as.zoo(cbind(rend_lvmh,-VaR_GARCH)),
  screens=1,
  col = c("black","lightseagreen"),
  main="VaR ARMA-GARCH(1,1)", 
  xlab = "Temps", 
  ylab = "Rendements"
)
points(as.zoo(rend_lvmh[as.numeric(rend_lvmh) < -VaR_GARCH]), pch=20, col="red")
legend("bottom",c("Rendements","VaR GARCH", "Dépassements de la VaR"),
         col = c("black","lightseagreen", 'red'),lty = c(1,1,NA),lwd = c(2,2,NA),pch = c(NA,NA,20))
garch11_estimation_fit <- 
  rugarch::ugarchfit(
    garch11_spec, 
    rend_lvmh, 
    out.sample = n_backtest 
  )

forecast_garch11 <- 
  rugarch::ugarchforecast(
    garch11_estimation_fit, 
    data = rend_lvmh,
    n.roll = n_backtest-1,
    n.ahead = 1 
  )

VaR_GARCH_backtest <- -(as.numeric(quantile(forecast_garch11, probs = 0.01)))

test_VaR_GARCH<- 
  rugarch::VaRTest(
    alpha = t_couv, 
    actual = sample_backtest,
    VaR = -VaR_GARCH_backtest,
    conf.level = 0.95 
  )

tibble(
  name = names(unlist(test_VaR_GARCH)), 
  value = unlist(test_VaR_GARCH) 
) |> 
  print(n = 20)
# A tibble: 12 × 2
   name            value                            
   <chr>           <chr>                            
 1 expected.exceed 20                               
 2 actual.exceed   30                               
 3 uc.H0           Correct Exceedances              
 4 uc.LRstat       4.37849677769573                 
 5 uc.critical     3.84145882069412                 
 6 uc.LRp          0.0363950816720029               
 7 uc.Decision     Reject H0                        
 8 cc.H0           Correct Exceedances & Independent
 9 cc.LRstat       7.41028267304434                 
10 cc.critical     5.99146454710798                 
11 cc.LRp          0.0245967407041665               
12 cc.Decision     Reject H0                        
HIT <- if_else(sample_backtest < -VaR_GARCH_backtest, 1, 0)

HIT_centre <- HIT - 0.01

stats::Box.test(HIT_centre, lag = 5, type = "Ljung-Box")

    Box-Ljung test

data:  HIT_centre
X-squared = 7.8284, df = 5, p-value = 0.166
df <- 
  tibble(
    HIT_centre = HIT_centre,
    rend = sample_backtest,
    rend2 = sample_backtest**2
  )

ARDL::ardl(
  HIT_centre ~ rend + rend2,
  data = df,
  order = 5
) |>  
  summary()

egarch11_spec <- 
  rugarch::ugarchspec(
    variance.model = 
      list(
        model = "eGARCH", 
        garchOrder = c(1,1),
        variance.targeting = TRUE 
      ),
    mean.model = 
      list(
        armaOrder = c(ar,ma), 
        include.mean = TRUE 
      ),
    distribution.model = "sged" 
  )

egarch11_fit <- 
  rugarch::ugarchfit(
    egarch11_spec, 
    rend_lvmh 
  )

egarch11_fit

VaR_EGARCH <- -(as.numeric(quantile(egarch11_fit, probs = t_couv)))

plot(
  as.zoo(cbind(rend_lvmh,-VaR_EGARCH)),
  screens=1,
  col = c("black","lightseagreen"),
  main="VaR ARMA-EGARCH(1,1)", 
  xlab = "Temps", 
  ylab = "Rendements"
)
points(as.zoo(rend_lvmh[as.numeric(rend_lvmh) < -VaR_EGARCH]), pch=20, col="red")
legend("bottom",c("Rendements","VaR EGARCH", "Dépassements de la VaR"),
         col = c("black","lightseagreen", 'red'),lty = c(1,1,NA),lwd = c(2,2,NA),pch = c(NA,NA,20))
egarch11_estimation_fit <- 
  rugarch::ugarchfit(
    egarch11_spec, 
    rend_lvmh, 
    out.sample = n_backtest 
  )

forecast_egarch11 <- 
  rugarch::ugarchforecast(
    egarch11_estimation_fit, 
    data = rend_lvmh,
    n.roll = n_backtest-1,
    n.ahead = 1 
  )

VaR_EGARCH_backtest <- -(as.numeric(quantile(forecast_egarch11, probs = 0.01)))

test_VaR_EGARCH<- 
  rugarch::VaRTest(
    alpha = t_couv, 
    actual = sample_backtest,
    VaR = -VaR_EGARCH_backtest,
    conf.level = 0.95 
  )

tibble(
  name = names(unlist(test_VaR_EGARCH)), 
  value = unlist(test_VaR_EGARCH) 
) |> 
  print(n = 20)

egarch22_spec <- 
  rugarch::ugarchspec(
    variance.model = 
      list(
        model = "eGARCH", 
        garchOrder = c(2,2),
        variance.targeting = TRUE 
      ),
    mean.model = 
      list(
        armaOrder = c(ar,ma), 
        include.mean = TRUE 
      ),
    distribution.model = "sged" 
  )

egarch22_fit <- 
  rugarch::ugarchfit(
    egarch22_spec, 
    rend_lvmh 
  )

egarch22_fit

*---------------------------------*
*          GARCH Model Fit        *
*---------------------------------*

Conditional Variance Dynamics   
-----------------------------------
GARCH Model : eGARCH(2,2)
Mean Model  : ARFIMA(2,0,2)
Distribution    : sged 

Optimal Parameters
------------------------------------
        Estimate  Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|)
mu      0.000167    0.000162    1.0291 0.303447
ar1     1.152746    0.040758   28.2827 0.000000

Elapsed time : 3.452638 
VaR_EGARCH22 <- -(as.numeric(quantile(egarch22_fit, probs = t_couv)))

plot(
  as.zoo(cbind(rend_lvmh,-VaR_EGARCH22)),
  screens=1,
  col = c("black","lightseagreen"),
  main="VaR ARMA-EGARCH(2,2)", 
  xlab = "Temps", 
  ylab = "Rendements"
)
points(as.zoo(rend_lvmh[as.numeric(rend_lvmh) < -VaR_EGARCH22]), pch=20, col="red")
legend("bottom",c("Rendements","VaR EGARCH(2,2)", "Dépassements de la VaR"),
         col = c("black","lightseagreen", 'red'),lty = c(1,1,NA),lwd = c(2,2,NA),pch = c(NA,NA,20))
egarch22_estimation_fit <- 
  rugarch::ugarchfit(
    egarch22_spec, 
    rend_lvmh, 
    out.sample = n_backtest 
  )

forecast_egarch22 <- 
  rugarch::ugarchforecast(
    egarch22_estimation_fit, 
    data = rend_lvmh,
    n.roll = n_backtest-1,
    n.ahead = 1 
  )

VaR_EGARCH22_backtest <- -(as.numeric(quantile(forecast_egarch22, probs = 0.01)))

test_VaR_EGARCH22<- 
  rugarch::VaRTest(
    alpha = t_couv, 
    actual = sample_backtest,
    VaR = -VaR_EGARCH22_backtest,
    conf.level = 0.95 
  )

tibble(
  name = names(unlist(test_VaR_EGARCH22)), 
  value = unlist(test_VaR_EGARCH22) 
) |> 
  print(n = 20)
# A tibble: 12 × 2
   name            value                            
   <chr>           <chr>                            
 1 expected.exceed 20                               
 2 actual.exceed   33                               
 3 uc.H0           Correct Exceedances              
 4 uc.LRstat       7.13670995390553                 
 5 uc.critical     3.84145882069412                 
 6 uc.LRp          0.00755215918452179              
 7 uc.Decision     Reject H0                        
 8 cc.H0           Correct Exceedances & Independent
 9 cc.LRstat       13.0095513372679                 
10 cc.critical     5.99146454710798                 
11 cc.LRp          0.00149627638279803              
12 cc.Decision     Reject H0                        
HIT <- if_else(sample_backtest < -VaR_EGARCH22_backtest, 1, 0)

HIT_centre <- HIT - 0.01

stats::Box.test(HIT_centre, lag = 5, type = "Ljung-Box")

    Box-Ljung test

data:  HIT_centre
X-squared = 13.528, df = 5, p-value = 0.01891
df <- 
  tibble(
    HIT_centre = HIT_centre,
    rend = sample_backtest,
    rend2 = sample_backtest**2
  )

ARDL::ardl(
  HIT_centre ~ rend + rend2,
  data = df,
  order = 5
) |>  
  summary()

Time series regression with "ts" data:
Start = 6, End = 2000

Call:
dynlm::dynlm(formula = full_formula, data = data, start = start, 
    end = end)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-0.59603 -0.02628  0.00448  0.02283  0.85761 

Coefficients:
                  Estimate Std. Error t value             Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)      -0.006077   0.003072  -1.978              0.04807 *

---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 0.1045 on 1977 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.3147,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.3088 
F-statistic:  53.4 on 17 and 1977 DF,  p-value: < 0.00000000000000022
df_VaR <- 
  tibble(
    VaR_VCov = VaR_VCov,
    VaR_CFisher = VaR_CFisher,
    VaR_student = VaR_student, 
    VaR_GARCH= VaR_GARCH,
    VaR_EGARCH = VaR_EGARCH,
    VaR_EGARCH22 = VaR_EGARCH22
  ) |> 
  drop_na() 

df_VaR_long <- 
  df_VaR |>
  mutate(temps = row_number()) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -temps,
    names_to = "model",
    values_to = "VaR"
  ) |> 
  mutate(
    .by = temps,
    moyenne_VaR = mean(VaR)
  ) |> 
  mutate(
    ecart = (VaR - moyenne_VaR) / moyenne_VaR
  ) |> 
  summarize(
    .by = model,
    MRB = abs(mean(ecart))
  ) |> 
  print()
# A tibble: 6 × 2
  model          MRB
  <chr>        <dbl>
1 VaR_VCov     0.251
2 VaR_CFisher  0.301
3 VaR_student  0.546
4 VaR_GARCH    0.204
5 VaR_EGARCH   0.196
6 VaR_EGARCH22 0.196

Exercice 4 : Dow Jones

Quel modèle de VaR décidez-vous de prendre ? Est-il un bon modèle de VaR ? Si non comment l’améliorer ?

rm(list = ls())
source(here::here("02-codes", "utils", "setup.R"))
dj <- 
  pdfetch::pdfetch_YAHOO(
    "RMS.PA",
    fields = "adjclose",
    from = as.Date("2007-01-03"), 
    to = as.Date("2026-03-30") 
  )

rend_dj <- 
  na.omit((log(dj) - log(lag(dj))) * 100) 

ar <- 6
integration <- 0
ma <- 0

t_couv <- 0.01

n_backtest <- 2000

taille_sample_estimation <- length(rend_dj) - n_backtest

sample_estimation <- rend_dj[1:taille_sample_estimation]

sample_backtest <- rend_dj[(taille_sample_estimation+1):length(rend_dj)]
garch11_spec <- 
  rugarch::ugarchspec(
    variance.model = 
      list(
        model = "sGARCH", 
        garchOrder = c(1,1), 
        variance.targeting = FALSE 
      ),
    mean.model = 
      list(
        armaOrder = c(ar,ma), 
        include.mean = FALSE
      ),
    distribution.model = "norm" 
  )
garch11_fit <- 
  rugarch::ugarchfit(
    garch11_spec,
    rend_dj 
  )

garch11_fit

*---------------------------------*
*          GARCH Model Fit        *
*---------------------------------*

Conditional Variance Dynamics   
-----------------------------------
GARCH Model : sGARCH(1,1)
Mean Model  : ARFIMA(6,0,0)
Distribution    : norm

------------------------------------

  as.zoo() |> 
  plot()

  as.zoo() |> 
  plot()
egarch11_spec <- 
  rugarch::ugarchspec(
    variance.model = 
      list(
        model = "eGARCH", 
        garchOrder = c(1,1), 
        variance.targeting = FALSE 
      ),
    mean.model = 
      list(
        armaOrder = c(ar,ma), 
        include.mean = FALSE
      ),
    distribution.model = "sged" 
  )
egarch11_fit <- 
  rugarch::ugarchfit(
    egarch11_spec,
    rend_dj 
  )

egarch11_fit

gjr_garch11_spec <- 
  rugarch::ugarchspec(
    variance.model = 
      list(
        model = "gjrGARCH", 
        garchOrder = c(1,1), 
        variance.targeting = FALSE 
      ),
    mean.model = 
      list(
        armaOrder = c(ar,ma), 
        include.mean = FALSE
      ),
    distribution.model = "sstd" 
  )

  rugarch::ugarchfit(
    gjr_garch11_spec,
    rend_dj 
  )

------------------------------------
  group statistic p-value(g-1)
1    20     23.11       0.2327
2    30     40.89       0.0704
3    40     36.65       0.5775

Elapsed time : 1.022975 
rugarch::persistence(gjr_garch11_fit)
[1] 0.9969728
rugarch::halflife(gjr_garch11_fit)
[1] 228.6237
plot(gjr_garch11_fit, which = 9)
rugarch::sigma(gjr_garch11_fit) |> 
  as.zoo() |> 
  plot()
rugarch::residuals(gjr_garch11_fit, standardize = TRUE) |> 
  as.zoo() |> 
  plot()
tibble(
  criterion = c("Akaike", "Bayes", "Shibata", "Hannan-Quinn"),
  garch_11 = rugarch::infocriteria(garch11_fit) |> as.numeric(),
  egarch_11 = rugarch::infocriteria(egarch11_fit) |> as.numeric(),
  gjr_garch_11 = rugarch::infocriteria(gjr_garch11_fit) |> as.numeric()
)
# A tibble: 4 × 4
  criterion    garch_11 egarch_11 gjr_garch_11
  <chr>           <dbl>     <dbl>        <dbl>
1 Akaike           3.73      3.65         3.64
2 Bayes            3.75      3.66         3.66
3 Shibata          3.73      3.65         3.64
4 Hannan-Quinn     3.74      3.65         3.65
VaR_VCov <- (-(mean(rend_dj)+sd(rend_dj)*qnorm(t_couv))) |> print()
[1] 3.983421
plot(as.zoo(rend_dj),
      main = "Rendements et Value at Risk variance-covariance",
      xlab = "Temps",
      ylab = "Rendements")
  abline(h= -VaR_VCov, col="cornflowerblue")
  points(as.zoo(rend_dj[as.numeric(rend_dj) < -VaR_VCov]), pch=20, col="cornflowerblue")
  legend("topright", legend = c("VaR Vcov","Dépassements de la VaR Vcov"), 
         pch = c(NA,20), lty = c(1, NA), col = "cornflowerblue")
VaR_VCov_estimation <- (-(mean(sample_estimation)+sd(sample_estimation)*qnorm(t_couv)))

test_VaR_VCov <- 
  rugarch::VaRTest(
    alpha = t_couv, 
    actual = sample_backtest, 
    VaR = rep(-VaR_VCov_estimation, times = length(sample_backtest)),
    conf.level = 0.95 
  )

tibble(
  name = names(unlist(test_VaR_VCov)), 
  value = unlist(test_VaR_VCov) 
) |> 
  print(n = 20)
# A tibble: 12 × 2
   name            value                            
   <chr>           <chr>                            
 1 expected.exceed 20                               
 2 actual.exceed   29                               
 3 uc.H0           Correct Exceedances              
 4 uc.LRstat       3.59165748872128                 
 5 uc.critical     3.84145882069412                 
 6 uc.LRp          0.0580702969680549               
 7 uc.Decision     Fail to Reject H0                
 8 cc.H0           Correct Exceedances & Independent
 9 cc.LRstat       6.8480438258995                  
10 cc.critical     5.99146454710798                 
11 cc.LRp          0.0325811325936156               
12 cc.Decision     Reject H0                        
HIT_VCov <- 
  if_else(sample_backtest < -VaR_VCov_estimation, 1, 0)

HIT_centre_VCov <- HIT_VCov - t_couv

stats::Box.test(HIT_centre_VCov, lag = 5, type = "Ljung-Box")

    Box-Ljung test

data:  HIT_centre_VCov
X-squared = 24.903, df = 5, p-value = 0.0001454
df <- tibble(
  HIT_centre = HIT_centre_VCov, 
  rend = sample_backtest |> as.numeric(), 
  rend2 = sample_backtest**2 |> as.numeric()
) 

ARDL::ardl( 
  HIT_centre ~ rend + rend2, 
  data = df,
  order = 5 
) |> 
  summary()

VaR_GARCH11 <- -(as.numeric(quantile(garch11_fit, probs = t_couv)))

plot(
  as.zoo(cbind(rend_dj,-VaR_GARCH11)),
  screens=1,
  col = c("black","lightseagreen"),
  main="VaR ARMA-GARCH(1)", 
  xlab = "Temps", 
  ylab = "Rendements"
)
points(as.zoo(rend_dj[as.numeric(rend_dj) < -VaR_GARCH11]), pch=20, col="red")
legend("bottom",c("Rendements","VaR GARCH", "Dépassements de la VaR"),
         col = c("black","lightseagreen", 'red'),lty = c(1,1,NA),lwd = c(2,2,NA),pch = c(NA,NA,20))
garch11_estimation_fit <- 
  rugarch::ugarchfit(
    garch11_spec, 
    rend_dj, 
    out.sample = n_backtest 
  )

forecast_garch11 <- 
  rugarch::ugarchforecast(
    garch11_estimation_fit, 
    data = rend_dj,
    n.roll = n_backtest-1,
    n.ahead = 1 
  )

VaR_GARCH_backtest <- -(as.numeric(quantile(forecast_garch11, probs = 0.01)))

test_VaR_GARCH<- 
  rugarch::VaRTest(
    alpha = t_couv, 
    actual = sample_backtest,
    VaR = -VaR_GARCH_backtest,
    conf.level = 0.95 
  )

tibble(
  name = names(unlist(test_VaR_GARCH)), 
  value = unlist(test_VaR_GARCH) 
) |> 
  print(n = 20)

HIT <- if_else(sample_backtest < -VaR_GARCH_backtest, 1, 0)

HIT_centre <- HIT - 0.01

stats::Box.test(HIT_centre, lag = 5, type = "Ljung-Box")

    Box-Ljung test

X-squared = 10.16, df = 5, p-value = 0.07084

  tibble(
    HIT_centre = HIT_centre,
    rend = sample_backtest,
    rend2 = sample_backtest**2
  )

ARDL::ardl(
  HIT_centre ~ rend + rend2,
  data = df,
  order = 5
) |>  
  summary()

  as.zoo(cbind(rend_dj,-VaR_EGARCH11)),

    egarch11_spec,

  rugarch::ugarchforecast(
    egarch11_estimation_fit, 
    data = rend_dj,
    n.roll = n_backtest-1,
    n.ahead = 1

    alpha = t_couv,

VaR_GJR_GARCH11 <- -(as.numeric(quantile(gjr_garch11_fit, probs = t_couv)))

plot(
  as.zoo(cbind(rend_dj,-VaR_GJR_GARCH11)),
  screens=1,
  col = c("black","lightseagreen"),
  main="VaR ARMA-GJR-GARCH(1)", 
  xlab = "Temps", 
  ylab = "Rendements"
)

legend("bottom",c("Rendements","VaR GJR-GARCH", "Dépassements de la VaR"),
         col = c("black","lightseagreen", 'red'),lty = c(1,1,NA),lwd = c(2,2,NA),pch = c(NA,NA,20))
gjr_garch11_estimation_fit <- 
  rugarch::ugarchfit(
    gjr_garch11_spec, 
    rend_dj, 
    out.sample = n_backtest 
  )

forecast_gjr_garch11 <- 
  rugarch::ugarchforecast(
    gjr_garch11_estimation_fit, 
    data = rend_dj,
    n.roll = n_backtest-1,
    n.ahead = 1 
  )

VaR_GJR_GARCH_backtest <- -(as.numeric(quantile(forecast_gjr_garch11, probs = 0.01)))

test_VaR_GJR_GARCH<- 
  rugarch::VaRTest(
    alpha = t_couv, 
    actual = sample_backtest,
    VaR = -VaR_GJR_GARCH_backtest,
    conf.level = 0.95 
  )

  name = names(unlist(test_VaR_GJR_GARCH)), 
  value = unlist(test_VaR_GJR_GARCH) 
) |>

df_VaR <- 
  tibble(
    VaR_VCov = VaR_VCov,
    VaR_GARCH11 = VaR_GARCH11,
    VaR_EGARCH11 = VaR_EGARCH11,
    VaR_GJR_GARCH11 = VaR_GJR_GARCH11
  ) |> 
  drop_na() 

df_VaR_long <- 
  df_VaR |>
  mutate(temps = row_number()) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -temps,
    names_to = "model",
    values_to = "VaR"
  ) |> 
  mutate(
    .by = temps,
    moyenne_VaR = mean(VaR)
  ) |> 
  mutate(
    ecart = (VaR - moyenne_VaR) / moyenne_VaR
  ) |> 
  summarize(
    .by = model,
    MRB = abs(mean(ecart))
  ) |> 
  print()
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  model              MRB
  <chr>            <dbl>
1 VaR_VCov        0.0106
2 VaR_GARCH11     0.100 
3 VaR_EGARCH11    0.0406
4 VaR_GJR_GARCH11 0.0491
